Switchyard

Shared provider runtime for AI apps.

Current truth:
- Supports BYOK + Web/Login as the only V1 supply lanes.
- Stable repo identity is `shared provider runtime for AI apps`, not a chat product, hosted control plane, or raw upstream fork.
- Primary repo-native surfaces today are the service/runtime front door and the read-only MCP descriptor at `packages/surfaces/mcp/server.json`.
- Current outward claims stay at `partial thin compat`, `partial read-only MCP surface`, `copy-ready starter packs`, and a docs-first builder front door.
- Live verification depends on local end-user credentials and browser session materials on the current machine.
- Live verification is workstation-bound. Any `verify:*live` or `reality:gate` result is proof/runbook truth for one credentialed environment, not permanent public identity.

Compatibility status:
- Codex compatibility: public `partial`; thin fail-closed runtime adapter is landed, but there is no tool/MCP/worktree parity claim.
- Claude Code compatibility: public `partial`; thin fail-closed runtime adapter is landed, but there is no tool/MCP/terminal parity claim.
- OpenClaw compatibility: public `partial`; thin fail-closed runtime adapter is landed, but there is no product-shell or operator parity claim.
- CLI starter: public `partial`; read-only builder/runtime helper is landed, but it is not an execution brain or distribution commitment.
- MCP adapter/server surface: public `partial`; a committed read-only MCP server/tool surface is landed, but it is not an execution brain and not a full consumer-parity backend.

First-row public routes:
- README.md
- docs/index.html
- docs/README.md
- docs/media/30-second-overview.md

- docs/mcp.md
- docs/compat/README.md
- docs/discoverability-keyword-truth.md
- catalogs/discoverability-keyword-truth.json

- docs/faq.md
- docs/glossary.md
- docs/i18n.md
